prestazioni: Replace the factory 426 Hemi (engine code: A102) with a fully built, forged internals version featuring a Callies DragonSlayer crankshaft, Manley H-beam connecting rods, and JE 12.5:1 compression pistons, paired with a custom camshaft grind from Comp Cams (256°/262° duration, .650/.650 lift) for optimized high-RPM power delivery on 116-octane race fuel.

Install a custom-built, dry-sump oiling system from Aviaid with a 10-quart reservoir and dual Gerotor pumps to ensure consistent lubrication under sustained lateral G-forces, critical for NASCAR oval racing where the Superbird originally competed.

Upgrade the factory 4-speed manual transmission (A-833) with a Tremec TKO-600 5-speed overdrive unit, reinforced with a McLeod twin-disc clutch (RST 10.5-inch) and a Hurst Competition Plus shifter for precise gear engagement, while retaining the original Dana 60 rear end but swapping in a Detroit Locker differential with 4.10:1 gearing for drag strip launches.
estetica: Replace the iconic but fragile fiberglass nose cone with a carbon-fiber replica from AAR (All American Racers), featuring integrated NACA ducts for brake cooling and a custom clear-coat finish with metallic flakes to match the original Petty Blue (PPG code: 426-1).

Install a custom, bolt-on widebody kit from Legendary Motorcar Company, designed specifically for the Superbird’s chassis, extending the rear fenders by 3 inches to accommodate 18x12-inch BBS LM wheels with 295/40R18 Mickey Thompson ET Street R tires, while retaining the original rear wing but reinforcing it with internal aluminum gussets for structural integrity.

Fabricate a custom, period-correct livery inspired by the 1970 NASCAR season, featuring hand-painted gold leaf accents on the hood scoop and rear wing, along with a retrofitted LED lighting system from United Pacific Industries that mimics the original sealed-beam headlights but with modern HID projectors for improved visibility.
